Title: MYB72-dependent coumarin exudation shapes root microbiome assembly to promote plant health
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron mobilization in the rhizosphere; antimicrobial activity shaping root microbiome assembly; suppression of soil-borne fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the glycosidic bond of scopolin to release the aglycone scopoletin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for scopoletin exudation, not established as overall pathway rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-expressed; induced downstream of MYB72 and associated with iron deficiency responses and beneficial rhizobacteria
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots under iron starvation and during rhizobacteria-induced systemic resistance context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: iron starvation; colonization by Pseudomonas simiae WCS417
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant metabolite profiling of root exudates and roots; HPLC-DAD quantification; UPLC-ESI-TOF-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes conversion of scopolin to scopoletin and is required for efficient scopoletin exudation into the rhizosphere
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bglu42 mutants showed strongly reduced scopoletin in root exudates under iron starvation. 2. bglu42 roots accumulated elevated scopolin, supporting impaired deglycosylation of scopolin. 3. BGLU42 is specifically important for processing scopolin into scopoletin before exudation into the rhizosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bglu42 blocks ISR competence and impairs exudation of iron-mobilizing coumarin scopoletin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering rhizosphere coumarin exudation, iron acquisition, and microbiome-mediated disease protection; should be coordinated with upstream MYB72 activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant produced near-wild-type levels of coumarins in roots, but exudation defect was specific and strongest for scopoletin; esculin/esculetin exudation was not affected similarly.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron mobilization in alkaline/iron-limiting environments; antimicrobial modulation of rhizosphere microbiota and fungal suppression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ortho-hydroxylation of feruloyl-CoA in the scopoletin biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for scopoletin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root iron-deficiency marker induced under iron starvation and by beneficial rhizobacteria context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots under iron starvation; coumarin-producing conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: iron starvation; limed soil maintaining iron limitation; colonization by Pseudomonas simiae WCS417
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scopoletin biosynthesis mutant analysis; fluorescence phenotyping; metabolite profiling; microbiome shotgun metagenome sequencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for scopoletin biosynthesis and thereby for coumarin-dependent shaping of the root microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. f6'h1 mutants failed to exude fluorescent coumarins under iron starvation. 2. f6'h1 root-associated microbiomes diverged strongly from wild type, indicating that F6'H1-dependent coumarins shape microbiome assembly. 3. WCS417-induced root coumarin production also occurred in an F6'H1-dependent manner.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of F6'H1 lowers microbial alpha diversity and abolishes scopoletin-mediated suppression of sensitive microbes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering coumarin-mediated iron acquisition and selective microbiome recruitment under iron-limiting soils
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: UPLC-ESI-TOF-MS metabolomics (Dataset S6); shotgun metagenome sequencing of root-associated microbiota (Table S3, Dataset S5)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as the scopoletin biosynthesis mutant benchmark linking coumarin biosynthesis to microbiome assembly rather than detailed enzymology in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron mobilization; root microbiome assembly; antimicrobial defense against soil-borne pathogens; supports plant health and induced systemic resistance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: coumarin biosynthesis and exudation associated with iron-deficiency response and ISR onset
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates BGLU42 and promotes expression of F6'H1 and iron-deficiency/coumarin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator required for production of major exuded coumarins, especially scopoletin, under iron starvation and beneficial microbe interaction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-specific; induced by iron starvation and colonization by ISR-inducing beneficial microbes such as Pseudomonas simiae WCS417
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots during iron deficiency and early after transplantation to limed soil; induced in rhizobacteria-colonized roots
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: iron starvation; limed soil; colonization by Pseudomonas simiae WCS417 and other ISR-inducing microbes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb72 loss-of-function mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of exudates and roots; HPLC-DAD quantification; microbiome shotgun metagenome sequencing
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: iron deficiency; beneficial rhizobacteria colonization; ISR-associated signals
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for coumarin biosynthesis/exudation, especially scopoletin accumulation in root exudates, thereby influencing root microbiome assembly and plant health
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. myb72 mutants lacked iron starvation-induced exudation of fluorescent coumarins, including scopoletin. 2. MYB72 was required for production of coumarins in roots and exudates, with scopoletin as the dominant MYB72-dependent exuded metabolite. 3. Loss of MYB72 altered root exudate metabolome and affected root-associated microbial community composition.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: myb72 mutants are defective in rhizobacteria-induced systemic resistance and coumarin-mediated iron-deficiency responses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator to engineer beneficial microbiome recruitment, iron acquisition, and ISR, but deployment should consider redundancy with MYB10 and broader immunity/iron-homeostasis effects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: UPLC-ESI-TOF-MS metabolomics (Datasets S1-S4, S6); shotgun metagenome sequencing (Table S3, Dataset S5)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Differences between Col-0 and myb72 microbiomes were subtler than between Col-0 and f6'h1, likely because MYB72 can function redundantly with MYB10 under iron-starved conditions.
